ucs.title: [LLM] APT41 web-facing app exploitation chain — IIS/Tomcat/Java spawning LOLBin recon (China Chopper / Log4Shell / ADSelfService Plus)
ucs.description: Hunts the post-exploitation behaviour APT41 (Wicked Panda / Brass Typhoon / BARIUM) consistently exhibits after exploiting public-facing web apps — Citrix NetScaler, Confluence (CVE-2022-26134), ManageEngine ADSelfService Plus (CVE-2021-40539), and Log4Shell — where a web-server worker process (w3wp/Tomcat/Java/nginx) spawns LOLBin reconnaissance utilities, the unmistakable footprint of China Chopper/Behinder webshell command execution. Mandiant's APT41 reporting and CISA AA22-238A attribute this exact chain to the group.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as command values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.process_path) as child_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat.exe","tomcat9.exe","tomcat10.exe","java.exe","javaw.exe","nginx.exe","node.exe")) (Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","quser.exe","tasklist.exe","wmic.exe","netstat.exe","reg.exe","schtasks.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","ping.exe","arp.exe","nltest.exe")) by host Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.user Processes.process
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
| sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat.exe","tomcat9.exe","tomcat10.exe","java.exe","javaw.exe","nginx.exe","node.exe")
| where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","quser.exe","tasklist.exe","wmic.exe","netstat.exe","reg.exe","schtasks.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","ping.exe","arp.exe","nltest.exe")
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd    = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildProcess = FileName,
          ChildCmd     = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildPath    = FolderPath,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| extend ParentImageName = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| extend ChildImageName  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,    "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentImageName in ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat.exe","tomcat9.exe","tomcat10.exe","java.exe","javaw.exe","nginx.exe","node.exe")
| where ChildImageName  in ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","quser.exe","tasklist.exe","wmic.exe","netstat.exe","reg.exe","schtasks.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","ping.exe","arp.exe","nltest.exe")
| where Account !endswith "$"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT41 Web-Server Process Spawning LOLBin Reconnaissance (China Chopper / Log4Shell post-exploitation)
id: 9b3a7c2e-4f15-4d4f-8a5d-c01a6f9b9e21
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects APT41 / Wicked Panda / Brass Typhoon post-exploitation tradecraft where
  IIS, Tomcat, Java or nginx worker processes spawn Windows LOLBins for host /
  domain reconnaissance — the canonical fingerprint of China Chopper or
  Behinder webshell command execution after exploiting Confluence (CVE-2022-26134),
  ManageEngine ADSelfService Plus (CVE-2021-40539), Log4Shell, or Citrix NetScaler.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0096/
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa22-238a
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/apt41-us-state-governments
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
tags:
  - attack.initial-access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.g0096
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  parent_webserver:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\w3wp.exe'
      - '\httpd.exe'
      - '\tomcat.exe'
      - '\tomcat9.exe'
      - '\tomcat10.exe'
      - '\java.exe'
      - '\javaw.exe'
      - '\nginx.exe'
      - '\node.exe'
  child_lolbin:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\netstat.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
      - '\schtasks.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
  condition: parent_webserver and child_lolbin
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate web app maintenance / health-check scripts (rare on production)
  - Application installers running under the IIS app-pool identity
level: high
ucs.rationale: Pinned tightly to APT41's documented operational pattern: every public Mandiant and CISA report on the group describes web-app exploitation followed by webshell-driven recon. The parent⇢child predicate eliminates the bulk of normal IIS/Java traffic — production web tiers should virtually never spawn whoami/net/nltest. Differentiates from generic T1059 process detection by requiring a *web-server p

ucs.title: [LLM] APT41 / Winnti DLL side-loading — signed legitimate binary executing from user-writable directory
ucs.description: Hunts the Winnti / APT41 / Blackfly hallmark of dropping a renamed, validly-signed third-party binary (commonly from VLC, Kaspersky, McAfee, VMware, Cisco, Adobe, NVIDIA installers) into a user-writable folder so it side-loads a malicious DLL from the same directory — the loader chain behind ShadowPad, PlugX, Crosswalk, KEYPLUG and Winnti backdoor variants documented by Symantec, Mandiant and ESET.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as command values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_path IN ("*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\PerfLogs\\*","*\\Windows\\Temp\\*","*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*","*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*","*\\Users\\Default\\*","*\\$Recycle.Bin\\*")) (Processes.process_name IN ("DTHelper.exe","vlc.exe","goopdate.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe","cookie_exporter.exe","RasTls.exe","wsc_proxy.exe","TmDbgLog.exe","corecmd.exe","mscorsvw.exe","vmnat.exe","vmware.exe","vmtoolsd.exe","mcods.exe","javaws.exe","NisSrv.exe","MsMpEng.exe","AcroRd32.exe","nvSmartMaxApp.exe","chrome.exe","OneDrive.exe")) by host Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.parent_process_name Processes.user
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
| sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// APT41 / Winnti side-load: signed third-party loader executing from a user-writable
// directory AND loading a DLL from the same directory whose hash differs from the loader.
let _suspect_dirs = dynamic([@"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\PerfLogs\",
                             @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\",
                             @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Users\Default\",
                             @"\$Recycle.Bin\"]);
let _legit_vendors = dynamic(["Microsoft","Google","VMware","Kaspersky","Avast",
                              "McAfee","Symantec","Bitdefender","Adobe","NVIDIA",
                              "Oracle","Realtek","Cisco","Citrix","Avira","BugSplat",
                              "Trend Micro","Sophos","VideoLAN","Notepad++"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (_suspect_dirs)
| where InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has_any (_legit_vendors)
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
| extend LoaderDir = tostring(parse_path(InitiatingProcessFolderPath).DirectoryPath)
| extend DllDir    = tostring(parse_path(FolderPath).DirectoryPath)
| where DllDir =~ LoaderDir                                  // sibling DLL — the side-load signal
| where SHA256 != InitiatingProcessSHA256                   // not the loader itself
| where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\System32\"
   and FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\SysWOW64\"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName,
          Loader        = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          LoaderPath    = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          LoaderVendor  = InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName,
          LoaderSHA256  = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          DllLoaded     = FileName,
          DllPath       = FolderPath,
          DllSHA256     = SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Requires Sysmon EID 7 (ImageLoad) ingestion via WindowsEvent / AMA.
let _suspect_dirs = dynamic([@"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\PerfLogs\",
                             @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\",
                             @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Users\Default\"]);
let _legit_vendors = dynamic(["Microsoft","Google","VMware","Kaspersky","Avast",
                              "McAfee","Symantec","Bitdefender","Adobe","NVIDIA",
                              "Oracle","Realtek","Cisco","Citrix","Trend Micro",
                              "Sophos","VideoLAN","Notepad++"]);
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon"
| where EventID == 7                                          // ImageLoad
| extend Image       = tostring(EventData.Image),
         ImageLoaded = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
         Company     = tostring(EventData.Company),
         Signed      = tostring(EventData.Signed),
         Hashes      = tostring(EventData.Hashes)
| where Signed =~ "true"
| where Company has_any (_legit_vendors)
| where Image has_any (_suspect_dirs)
| where ImageLoaded endswith ".dll"
| extend LoaderDir = strcat_array(array_slice(split(Image,       "\\"), 0, -2), "\\"),
         LoadedDir = strcat_array(array_slice(split(ImageLoaded, "\\"), 0, -2), "\\")
| where tolower(LoaderDir) == tolower(LoadedDir)
| where ImageLoaded !startswith @"C:\Windows\System32\"
   and  ImageLoaded !startswith @"C:\Windows\SysWOW64\"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Image, Company, ImageLoaded, Hashes
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: DLL side-loading is the single most-cited APT41/Winnti/Blackfly TTP across vendor reporting (Mandiant M-Trends, Symantec Threat Hunter Team, ESET Winnti reports, Microsoft Brass Typhoon writeups). Generic side-loading detections fire on legitimate installers; this rule narrows the field by requiring (a) a signed third-party vendor binary (b) running from a user-writable path that vendors NEVER shi

_field_issues.message: `InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName` is not a column of DeviceImageLoadEvents; it does exist on DeviceProcessEvents, DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceFileEvents.
